home.heroSubtitle: An educational anti-bullying project by Buga Victoria, psychologist — practical, science-based tools for students, parents and teachers.
home.presentationDesc: A short presentation that introduces the project and its core message — for children, and for everyone who cares about them.
home.motherHeading: With a specialist's mind and a mother's heart
home.motherP1: I am the mother of three children, and the thought that one of them could become a victim of humiliation, exclusion or emotional aggression drives me to understand this phenomenon and help prevent it. Every child deserves to feel safe, accepted and respected in the environment where they learn and grow. I study this subject with the soul of a specialist, but also with the heart of a mother. I wish for no child to ever feel alone, unheard or unprotected.
home.motherP2: Together we can turn school and community into a space where every child has the courage to be themselves.
home.motherImageAlt: Buga Victoria's three children in a spring, flower-filled setting
home.audienceSubtitle: Choose your space. Every resource is tailored to you.

student.desc: You are stronger than you think. Discover videos, games and a letter written just for you.

parent.desc: Guides and a 24-hour action plan to help you protect and support your child.

teacher.desc: Practical techniques and lesson materials to build a safe classroom.

home.missionIntro: “Bullying, explained for everyone” is not just a title — it is a personal mission.
home.quote: A life is worth nothing — but nothing is worth as much as a life.

prevent.title: Prevent
prevent.desc: Understanding the psychological roots of both the aggressor and the victim is the first step toward breaking this cycle.

protect.title: Protect
protect.desc: Children and teenagers need healthy coping mechanisms and a scientifically validated support system.

educate.title: Educate
educate.desc: Through accurate information we can turn passive “bystanders” into active allies of the vulnerable.

about.title: About me
about.subtitle: Who am I?
about.intro: I am Buga Victoria — daughter, psychologist, wife, mother, colleague, friend…
about.body: I chose to become a psychologist out of a deep conviction that every person deserves to be heard, understood and supported on their journey of becoming. In an increasingly noisy world, I felt the need to create a space of calm and safety for the human soul. My fascination with the mechanisms of the mind and my desire to transform suffering into resilience guided me toward this profession, which is, in essence, a form of the art of human connection.
about.whyIntro: I chose the topic of bullying for my research because I believe it is one of the most critical challenges of today's educational and social environment. “Bullying, explained for everyone” is not just a title, but a personal mission to:
about.quote: A life is worth nothing — but nothing is worth as much as a life.
about.quoteCaption: This quote reflects the essence of my work: the fight for the dignity and balance of every person who crosses the threshold of the office or who is part of our community.
about.portraitAlt: Black-and-white portrait of a mother holding her smiling child

project.title: The project
project.subtitle: Bullying, explained for everyone
project.whyIntro: I chose the topic of bullying for my research because I believe it is one of the most critical challenges of today's educational and social environment. “Bullying, explained for everyone” is not just a title, but a personal mission to:
project.goal: The goal of this project is to raise awareness of, prevent and combat bullying among students and young people by offering psychological tools accessible to everyone involved: victims, aggressors, parents and teachers. The project aims to turn the educational environment into a safe space built on empathy and mutual respect.

objectives.title: Inform & clarify
objectives.objective: Identify and explain the main forms of bullying (physical, verbal, social and cyberbullying) through at least 5 educational resources (articles, infographics or presentations) posted in the portfolio.
objectives.meta: Timeframe: within the first 4 weeks of the project.

objectives.title: Develop empathy
objectives.objective: Create and distribute a school-climate assessment questionnaire for a group of at least 30 students, to measure the level of empathy and the incidence of aggressive behaviour.
objectives.meta: Timeframe: over the course of one school semester.

objectives.title: Intervene & support
objectives.objective: Develop a practical “Emotional First Aid” guide containing 5 coping techniques for bullying victims and 3 anger-management strategies for aggressors.
objectives.meta: Timeframe: by the completion of the current research.

objectives.title: Social relevance
objectives.objective: Organise or propose a group counselling session (online or in person) addressing the central quote: “A life is worth nothing — nothing is worth as much as a life”, emphasising the intrinsic value of every child.
objectives.meta: Relevance: essential for reducing the risk of school dropout and depression.

objectives.title: Monitor impact
objectives.objective: Evaluate the impact of the information presented in the portfolio by collecting at least 10 testimonials or pieces of feedback from visitors (students or colleagues) by the end of the academic year.
objectives.meta: Timeframe: by the end of the academic year.

project.realText: This portfolio became the foundation of the international educational project “Unity through Diversity — Saying NO to bullying through tradition”, a partnership between the Republic of Moldova and the Czech Republic.
project.realCta: See the collaboration with the Czech Republic

students.heroTitle: You are stronger than you think. Welcome!
students.heroSubtitle: This is your safe space — explore it with confidence.
students.letterImageAlt: Illustrated letter to students: “Welcome to your safe space! Hi! I'm so glad you made it here…”

doc_techniques.title: Anti-Bullying Techniques for Students
doc_techniques.desc: A presentation with practical techniques to protect yourself and react calmly in bullying situations.

letter.p1: Hi! I'm so glad you made it here. This corner of the internet was created especially for you. I know that sometimes school or your group of friends can become complicated places, and bullying can make you feel alone or misunderstood. I want you to know that here, your voice matters.
letter.p2: You are not alone in this story. Here you'll find advice on how to become stronger, how to help others and — most importantly — you'll learn that a life is worth nothing, and nothing is worth as much as a life. That means YOUR life is the most precious thing. Explore with confidence!

students.videosDesc: 📢 Watch short clips that will help you understand the situation correctly, judge it well and know how to react next. Choose the clip that resonates with you, depending on your age. 🎞️
students.game1Desc: 📌 I can help you understand the idea of bullying better — through a game. Follow the steps below… 🌟
students.game2Desc: 🧲 If you enjoyed the experience above, let's keep practising through play… 🪂
students.gameNote: If the game doesn't load below, use the button to open it in a new tab.
game1Instructions: Tap the card to see if you answered correctly…
game2Instructions: Decide: is it bullying or friendship?
Drag the card into the right column…
students.quote: If you are different, it means you have something special to offer the world. Don't try to fit into a crowd that doesn't deserve you.

gallery.hero: A child sitting alone in the school hallway
gallery.fingers: A child surrounded by pointing fingers
gallery.filming: A child being filmed and mocked by classmates
gallery.girls: A girl being laughed at by a group
gallery.stop: A child holding up a hand that says “stop”
gallery.illustration: Illustration of a child being laughed at

parents.title: For parents
parents.welcome: Welcome! Read the letter of intent.
parents.quote: A child who feels loved and respected at home will rarely need to hurt someone at school — and will always have the courage to ask for help if they are hurt.
parents.letterAlt: An open letter to parents, signed “With love, Buga Victoria”

letter.greeting: Hey, welcome to our super-parents club! 🤝
letter.p1: We all know that school should be a magical realm where our children discover their superpowers and make friends for life. But sometimes this realm is invaded by creatures called “bullying.”
letter.p2: Whether you're here because you're fighting a dragon alongside your child, or you want to be an informed and prepared knight, you've come to exactly the right place! This page was created to turn helplessness into action and silence into heroic dialogue.
bullets: Magical resources & strategies: how to spot the subtle signs of bullying (physical, verbal or online).
A communication guide: how to talk to your child without making them feel interrogated by a secret agent.
Concrete steps: how to form an alliance with the school and handle conflicts like a Jedi master.
Support: a community that knows there are no “bad children” — only behaviours that need a little magic and a few boundaries.
letter.p3: Remember: bullying is not a “rite of passage”, nor something children must “endure” to become stronger. It is a matter of emotional safety that concerns us all.
letter.p4: Let's explore the solutions together, ask questions and build a safer realm for our children's future. You are not alone in this adventure!

parents.closing: No one is guaranteed that their own child won't end up on the negative side — don't despair, you can always help them.

doc_phase.title: “Just a phase” or is it Bullying? — How to decode a child's silence
doc_phase.desc: 🚑 A guide on how to give quick help without harming your child. Read it first.

doc_24h.title: The 24-hour plan FOR PARENTS
doc_24h.desc: An action plan for the first day after bullying. The first 24 hours are essential — read carefully and choose exactly how to react promptly, for your child's benefit.

doc_my_child.title: My child is the one doing the bullying
doc_my_child.desc: “We are parents, not perfect. How do we help our children become better?”

doc_activities.title: Activities for children who bully
doc_activities.desc: Try these activities and stay by their side. Support is the most important step here — you can turn a moral lesson into attractive games with quick results.

evaluation.title: Project evaluation
evaluation.subtitle: Your feedback matters
evaluation.intro: This project grows with your voice. Share a testimonial, an impression or a suggestion — your feedback helps measure the project's impact and improve the resources for students, parents and teachers.
evaluation.quizIntro: A short quiz on what the project teaches about bullying. Pick one answer per question, then check your results.
evaluation.quizPerfect: Perfect score — you're a true ally against bullying! 🎉
evaluation.quizGood: Well done! You know the essentials. 👏
evaluation.quizKeep: Good start — explore the pages and try again! 💪
evaluation.quizAnswerAll: Answer all questions to see your results.

quiz.q: Which stress hormone is experienced by both the victim and the aggressor?
quiz.explanation: Cortisol is the main stress hormone, felt by both sides involved.

quiz.q: What is the project's central quote?
options: “A life is worth nothing — nothing is worth as much as a life.”
quiz.explanation: It underlines the intrinsic value of every child.

quiz.q: What does “Save” mean in the 3 “S” rule for cyberbullying?
quiz.explanation: Save the evidence (screenshots) before reporting a case of cyberbullying.

quiz.q: What is the role of an “Upstander”?
options: Someone who takes a stand and becomes a defender
Someone who ignores the differences between pupils
quiz.explanation: An upstander takes action and defends the vulnerable, unlike a passive bystander.

quiz.q: What are the main forms of bullying?
quiz.explanation: Bullying can be physical, verbal, social (relational) and online (cyberbullying).

quiz.q: Which country was the “Unity through Diversity” project partnered with?
quiz.explanation: The project is a partnership between the Republic of Moldova and the Czech Republic.

evaluation.name: Your name
evaluation.messagePlaceholder: Write your impression, testimonial or suggestion here…
evaluation.note: The button opens your email app with the message ready to send to Buga Victoria.

czech.title: Unity through Diversity
czech.subtitle: Saying NO to bullying through tradition
czech.intro: An international educational project that prevents and combats bullying in primary school by turning cultural diversity into a tool for connection — developing children's emotional intelligence and spirit of tolerance through national traditions and folklore.
czech.goal: To build and apply a model for preventing and combating bullying in schools by valuing cultural diversity as a tool for cohesion, within an international educational partnership (Moldova – Czech Republic), through the celebration of national traditions and customs.
czech.argument: Bullying is no longer an isolated incident but a systemic, global problem. Children increasingly struggle to accept diversity and the unknown, and up to 50% of pupils moving from primary to secondary school experience bullying. It is far more effective to prevent and educate from the start than to correct deeply rooted patterns later. We chose a partner school in the Czech Republic for its vast experience in school inclusion and pupil well-being — letting us compare prevention methods and adopt good European practices.

stages.title: “My heritage – your heritage”
stages.desc: An anti-bullying team of 4th-grade pupils was trained and ran awareness sessions in 10 classes and 2 kindergarten groups. Children met online, shared their most beautiful traditions, exchanged letters and postcards, and wrote an anti-bullying manifesto.
stages.motto: Do not do to others what you would not want done to you.

stages.title: “Click on Kindness”
stages.desc: A digital winter-customs gathering: pupils learned carols and folk dances symbolising unity, composed bilingual anti-bullying proverbs and created a bilingual e-book of kind phrases.

stages.title: “The Thread of Friendship – bilingual Mărțișor”
stages.desc: Starting from the legend of the Mărțișor, children created bilingual mărțișoare with kind words and held a joint exhibition. The Czech pupils wore them in March as a sign of friendship — proof that true friendship knows no borders.

stages.title: “Different but United”
stages.desc: Through Easter traditions of forgiveness, generosity and peace, children learned that diversity is not a reason for marginalisation but a richness that unites us.

quant: 72 pupils from Moldova and Czechia took active part in all the workshops
252 primary pupils reached by the anti-bullying prevention campaign
7 teachers involved (3 from Moldova, 4 from Czechia)
8 joint online activities with the Czech partner class
1 digital portfolio and 1 international partnership agreement
qual: Greater resilience in the face of aggression
Assertive communication and clearly voiced boundaries
Therapy through tradition and collaborative activities
Teamwork that dismantled the power hierarchies typical of bullying
czech.gallerySubtitle: Moments from the joint activities of the children in Moldova and the Czech Republic.
czech.photoAlt: Activity from the Moldova × Czech Republic project
czech.closing: In this project we choose to be heroes of kindness, not spectators of cruelty. If you see bullying — don't stay silent, be a true friend! Together we build a school of friendship.

footer.blurb: An educational anti-bullying project offering science-based tools for students, parents and teachers.
footer.builtWith: Rebuilt as a modern, accessible web app.
